headline: CONFIRMATION with documented in-window EMP negative signal. April 23 Bloomberg/CNBC: Meta announced 8,000 employee layoffs (10% workforce) effective May 20 + cancellation of 6,000 open roles, framed as AI-efficiency-driven amid $115-135B 2026 capex. New Mexico jury verdict ($375M penalty for failing to protect young users from CSAM exploitation) and LA jury verdict ($6M for addictive product design) compound the existing child-safety scandal already absorbed in baseline 29.4. Net effect on composite is small: layoffs are EMP negative (-0.2), but child-safety verdicts confirm rather than newly add to BND/INT scoring. Composite 29.4 (Developing) holds; -0.7 calibration on EMP brings composite to 28.7 — within the 5-point threshold and below the calibration-class threshold for floor-adjacent entities. Confirm at 29.4.

evidence.finding: 8,000 layoffs (10% workforce), effective May 20. AI efficiency rationale. 6,000 open roles cancelled. Capex $115-135B for 2026 (vs $72B in 2025).
Independent corroboration of 8,000 layoffs and Meta Superintelligence Labs investment context.
New Mexico jury: $375M penalty potential for failing to protect young users from CSAM exploitation. LA jury: $6M for addictive product design. Establishes legal liability for known harms.
Combined 20K+ tech job cuts at Meta + Microsoft same week; AI labor displacement context.

methodologyNote: Calibration class proposal considered but not adopted. Layoffs justify EMP -0.2 calibration but child-safety verdicts (March 2026, out of window) are CONFIRMATORY rather than additive — already absorbed in current baseline. Composite at 29.4 already reflects mid-Developing band positioning consistent with current evidence stack. Sub-5pt threshold for proposal not crossed; no floor-limitation justification (Meta AI is at 29.4, expressive room exists upward and downward).
